proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e == 0`.
Cell lines with `lineage == "skin"` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than non-skin cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_BRAF for braf_v600e == 1 versus braf_v600e == 0.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_BRAF` was much more negative in `braf_v600e == 1` lines (mean -0.8047, n=5574) than in `braf_v600e == 0` lines (mean -0.2495, n=44426).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_BRAF for lineage == "skin" versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: `lineage == "skin"` also showed more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores (mean -0.4721, n=4935) than other lineages (mean -0.2938), consistent with enrichment of BRAF-driven models.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 0`.
Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 0`.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_EGFR by egfr_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `egfr_amplification == 1` lines had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.6010 (n=3880) versus -0.2524 in non-amplified lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_ERBB2 by erbb2_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `erbb2_amplification == 1` lines had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.6856 (n=4890) versus -0.2535 in non-amplified lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `kras_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation == 0`.
Cell lines with `lineage == "pancreatic"` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than non-pancreatic cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KRAS by kras_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation == 1` lines had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.5594 (n=11842), compared with -0.2520 in KRAS-wild-type lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KRAS for lineage == "pancreatic" versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: Pancreatic lines had more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores (mean -0.4260, n=5102) than other lineages (mean -0.3133).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `nf1_loss == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss == 0`.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_RIT1 by nf1_loss.
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss == 1` lines had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.3527 (n=4560) versus -0.2572 in `nf1_loss == 0` lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `lineage == "colorectal"` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `apc_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation == 0`.
Cell lines with `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with lower `wnt_activity_score`.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KIF18A for lineage == "colorectal" versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: Colorectal lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.7204 (n=5816), while non-colorectal lines averaged -0.2524.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KIF18A by apc_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: `apc_mutation == 1` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.5447 (n=9285), versus -0.2526 in APC-wild-type lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KIF18A for wnt_activity_score >= 75th percentile versus lower WNT activity.
analyses.result_summary: High-WNT lines (`wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`) had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.4694 (n=12508), versus -0.2526 in lower-WNT lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup `lineage == "colorectal"`, `apc_mutation == 1`, `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`, and `smad4_loss == 0` has much more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
Within the colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT context, `smad4_loss == 1` suppresses the KIF18A dependency so that `dependency_KIF18A` is not more negative than in other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KIF18A for lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 AND smad4_loss == 0 versus all other rows.
analyses.result_summary: The refined subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2990 (n=2571), while all other cell lines averaged -0.2530.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KIF18A for lineage == "colorectal" AND apc_mutation == 1 AND wnt_activity_score >= 0.819 AND smad4_loss == 1 versus all other rows.
analyses.result_summary: The same colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT subgroup with `smad4_loss == 1` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.2557 (n=562), not a stronger dependency than the rest of the dataset (mean -0.3074).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `myc_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with `myc_amplification == 0`.
Cell lines with `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with lower `copy_number_8q24`.
Cell lines with both `myc_amplification == 1` and `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_RPL11 by myc_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification == 1` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3614 (n=8055), versus -0.2523 in non-amplified lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_RPL11 for copy_number_8q24 >= 75th percentile versus lower values.
analyses.result_summary: High `copy_number_8q24` lines had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3440 (n=12501), versus -0.2452 in lower-copy lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_RPL11 for myc_amplification == 1 AND copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428 versus all other rows.
analyses.result_summary: The MYC-amplified/high-8q24 subgroup had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3647 (n=7797), versus -0.2524 in all other cell lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than slower-growing cell lines.
Cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than slower-growing cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_POLR2A for growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 75th percentile versus lower growth rate.
analyses.result_summary: Fast-growing lines had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.4972 (n=12577), compared with -0.3928 in slower-growing lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_TMED10 for growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 75th percentile versus lower growth rate.
analyses.result_summary: Fast-growing lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.2816 (n=12577), compared with -0.2447 in slower-growing lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than cell lines with lower `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`.
Higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` is associated with more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_DHX9 for rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 75th percentile versus lower values.
analyses.result_summary: High-MYC-expression lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3551 (n=12501), while lower-MYC lines averaged -0.3265.

analyses.code: Linear association test of dependency_DHX9 on growth_rate_doublings_per_day.
analyses.result_summary: `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` was negatively associated with `dependency_DHX9`; the fitted slope was -0.1086 dependency-score units per additional doubling per day.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over single-feature and pairwise subgroups will identify `braf_v600e == 1` as the strongest subgroup for more negative `dependency_BRAF`.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over single-feature and pairwise subgroups will identify `egfr_amplification == 1` as the strongest subgroup for more negative `dependency_EGFR`.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over single-feature and pairwise subgroups will identify `erbb2_amplification == 1` as the strongest subgroup for more negative `dependency_ERBB2`.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over single-feature and pairwise subgroups will identify `kras_mutation == 1` as the strongest subgroup for more negative `dependency_KRAS`.
A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over single-feature and pairwise subgroups will identify `nf1_loss == 1` as the strongest subgroup for more negative `dependency_RIT1`.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over single-feature, pairwise, and refined multi-feature subgroups will identify `lineage == "colorectal"`, `apc_mutation == 1`, `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819`, and `smad4_loss == 0` as the strongest subgroup for more negative `dependency_KIF18A`.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over single-feature and pairwise subgroups will not find a strong molecular subgroup for `dependency_TMED10`; the leading signal will be the screen covariate `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` with more negative dependency.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over single-feature and pairwise subgroups will identify high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` or high growth rate as the leading subgroups for more negative `dependency_DHX9`.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over single-feature and pairwise subgroups will identify `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` as the strongest subgroup for more negative `dependency_POLR2A`.

A systematic dependency-heterogeneity search over single-feature and pairwise subgroups will identify `myc_amplification == 1` with high `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` as the strongest subgroup for more negative `dependency_RPL11`.

analyses.code: Systematic scan of all dependency outcomes against single-feature masks, continuous-feature quartile masks, and pairwise subgroup masks; KIF18A was additionally refined with a four-predicate subgroup after the pairwise scan.
analyses.result_summary: The screen recovered the dominant subgroups: BRAF with `braf_v600e == 1` (effect -0.5552), EGFR with `egfr_amplification == 1` (effect -0.3486), ERBB2 with `erbb2_amplification == 1` (effect -0.4321), KRAS with `kras_mutation == 1` (effect -0.3075), RIT1 with `nf1_loss == 1` (effect -0.0955), KIF18A with the colorectal/APC/high-WNT/SMAD4-intact subgroup (effect -1.0460), TMED10 with fast growth (effect -0.0369), DHX9 with high MYC expression and growth effects (high-MYC effect -0.0286; growth slope -0.1086), POLR2A with fast growth (effect -0.1043), and RPL11 with MYC amplification/high 8q24 copy number (effect -0.1123).
